name: Nova Cash Advance
description_short: Nova Cash Advance provides short-term loans funded directly to your checking account, typically within one business day, using your available credit card limit as collateral for the advance amount.
description_long:
Nova Cash Advance is a short-term lending service that operates by connecting to your existing credit card and providing a separate cash advance loan based on your available credit. The company deposits funds directly into your checking account, typically within one business day of approval, making it positioned as an emergency cash solution for immediate financial needs.

The service works by analyzing your linked credit card's available credit, applying a risk-based percentage factor (typically 10-30%), and determining your eligible advance amount. Repayment is handled through automatic ACH debits from the same checking account. The company charges a single-digit percentage fee on the advance amount plus an APR that typically ranges from the high teens to low thirties, depending on your card issuer and state regulations. Additional fees such as processing or late-payment charges may apply based on your specific loan terms.

Nova distinguishes itself through its rapid funding timeline (one business day or faster) and by not requiring a traditional credit application or hard pull—instead leveraging existing credit card relationships. The company provides transparent fee and APR disclosures within their app before you confirm the loan, and they include state-specific restrictions and issuer-specific caps in their terms.

While Nova offers speed and convenience for emergency cash needs, borrowers should be aware that the advance is a separate loan tied to their credit card with mandatory repayment via automatic debit. The APR range can be substantial, and missing payments may trigger late fees and impact credit standing. This product is most appropriate for those with existing credit cards and immediate cash needs who can repay within the stated terms.
pros: Funding typically arrives within one business day after approval, enabling rapid access to emergency cash
No traditional credit application required; uses existing credit card data to determine eligibility
Advance is independent of credit card limit—a separate loan that doesn't reduce your card's available credit
Fee and APR are disclosed in-app before you confirm the loan, allowing you to review exact costs upfront
Eligibility based on straightforward criteria (age 18+, active checking account, linked debit card, available credit)
Automatic ACH repayment removes need for manual payment processing
State-specific restrictions and issuer-specific caps are disclosed in loan terms before acceptance
cons: APR range (high teens to low thirties) can be substantially higher than traditional personal loans
Single-digit percentage fee plus APR means total cost of borrowing is significant for short-term needs
Mandatory automatic ACH debit withdrawal—missing payment triggers late fees and potential credit damage
Advance amount is capped at a percentage of available credit (10-30%), limiting access for those with low limits
Requires an active checking account with linked debit card, excluding unbanked or underbanked consumers
best_for: Employed individuals with active credit cards and valid checking accounts facing immediate cash emergencies
Borrowers who need funds faster than traditional personal loans (within one business day)
Those with sufficient available credit who want to avoid another hard credit inquiry
Consumers who prefer transparent, upfront fee disclosures before committing to a loan
diagnosis: Nova Cash Advance is best for employed individuals with active credit cards and checking accounts who face genuine emergencies and can repay within the stated term. The primary caveat is that the combination of single-digit percentage fees plus high-teens to low-thirties APR makes this an expensive form of borrowing; use only when speed is essential and other options are unavailable or slower.
services: Short-term cash advance loans funded to checking account within one business day
Risk-based advance amount calculation using available credit card data
Automatic ACH repayment debit processing from linked checking account
In-app fee and APR disclosure before loan confirmation
Issuer-specific cap application and cardholder agreement review
Pending purchase and authorization deduction from advance eligibility
Late payment fee assessment and reporting
Bank and personal data protection and security measures
Borrower scenario analysis and outcome documentation
Client login portal for account management
Free credit analysis and soft pull review (via The Credit People partnership)
